---
| | Hillary Clinton is adding her voice to our movement. She recently said that voter suppression has led to 'crisis in democracy' in the US. The Secretary of State is right: - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Hillary Clinton is adding her voice to our movement: [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d06ce/72412298/41de69e3/610353fc/962302144/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/hrc_thankyoucard?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190922_HRCThanks_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190922_HRCThanks_U1_X1") The Secretary of State is right: National Republicans are using their positions of power to prevent Democrats and people of color from voting, and we'll keep losing elections because of it. **[Add your name to thank Secretary Clinton for speaking out on this critical issue>>](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d06ce/72412298/41de69e3/610353ff/962302144/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/hrc_thankyoucard?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+thank+Secretary+Clinton&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190922_HRCThanks_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190922_HRCThanks_U1_X1")** | | | [Sign the card >> ](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d06ce/72412298/41de69e3/610353fe/962302144/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/hrc_thankyoucard?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+Sign+the+card+gtgt+&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190922_HRCThanks_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190922_HRCThanks_U1_X1")

Hillary Clinton is right -- and she isn't just talking about 2016, either:

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: GOP closes polling places and silences voters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter, After the Supreme Court ruled that a key part of the Voting Rights Act was unconstitutional in 2013, the floodgates opened for states to pass restrictions on voting rights that disenfranchise minority voters and help keep the GOP in power. In the six years since that fateful decision, GOP legislatures and election offices closed 1,688 poll sites in 13 states, with many closed sites clustered in the South. In Texas alone, they shut down 750 polling places. In some Texas counties, that's left upwards of 75% of polling stations closed \-- which is especially disastrous in rural countries where voters must take off work, drive long distances, and wait in long lines just to exercise their Constitutional right to vote. Expanding voting rights should be a goal no matter what party you belong to \-- yet Republican-controlled state legislatures are overseeing this sweeping dismantlement of voting rights across the country. They are gerrymandering their states' election maps, creating poll taxes, and blocking automatic voter registration, all in an attempt to pick and choose the electorate. Republicans know that their ideas aren't winning over any voters, so they now resort to silencing anyone who won't vote for them instead \-- and Democrats need to stand up to their blatant voter suppression. We need to rally and push back against the GOP's onslaught of attacks on democracy, and the only way we'll do this is vote them out and win these legislatures back. [**WDTO is ready to fight against the GOP for control of state legislatures, and we know they 're going to throw millions of dollars in dark money into every election they can, so we need your help. Please, every donation made, no matter how small, will go toward making true, lasting change in our elections. Could you support us with a donation of $1 today?**](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd05/6e9863e8/14f3deae/1db0dbc3/1404038714/VEsH/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/when-democrats-turn-out-nd?refcode=WDTO20190922a.ND-b&refcode2=e5k5fvCTF1AK3rtH00lY") Thank you for standing with us! -The WDTO Team [DONATE](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd05/6e9863e8/14f3deae/1db0dbc3/1404038714/VEsE/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/when-democrats-turn-out-nd?refcode=WDTO20190922a.ND-b&refcode2=e5k5fvCTF1AK3rtH00lY")

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Tina Smith is the key to a Democratic Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek,

Democrats have a real chance at taking back the Senate in 2020. I don't have to tell you how much of a game-changer that would be -- from protecting and expanding affordable health care to taking meaningful action to address the scourge of gun violence in our communities, a Democratic Senate is the key to forward progress for our country. That's why I'm writing you today. Tina Smith is running for re-election in Minnesota against a Trump-picked challenger who will be backed with all the special interest money they can stuff into his warchest. **[We need to fight back and make sure Tina stays in the Senate. Can you chip in $10 or more to help Tina Smith be a part of a Democratic majority in the Senate in 2020?](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1769/3e0906/78f728b7/3e30e5a3/2710231057/VEsH/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-ts?express_lane=true&refcode=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+We+need+to+fight+back+and+make+sure+Tina&utm_campaign=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS&source=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS")** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**DONATE $10**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1769/3e0906/78f728b7/3e30e5a2/2710231057/VEsE/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-ts?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS_10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS&source=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS")

Derek, the presidential election is getting a lot of attention. And that's a good thing, because taking back the White House is going to be a critical part of getting our country back on track. But I want to be as clear as I can possibly be: If we want to pass legislation that will improve the lives of Virginians and all Americans, we've got to have a Democratic Senate, too.
Tina needs us to have her back. **Please consider pitching in $10 or more before the end-of-September FEC filing deadline so we can defend Minnesota 's Senate seat.** The Republicans will have big checks from special interests, wealthy donors, and dark money organizations. But Tina has something that's just as powerful -- you!
******[**DONATE NOW**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1769/3e0906/78f728b7/3e30e5a6/2710231057/VEsA/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-ts?express_lane=true&refcode=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=6+-+DONATE+NOW&utm_campaign=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS&source=20190922_1_FR_TK_TS")******
---
This race couldn't be more important. We need Tina's voice, and her vote, in the U.S. Senate.
Thank you for everything you do,
-Tim Kaine

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: Ivanka promised childcare and support for working women
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter, Ivanka Trump was supposedly going to be the voice of reason in this White House, advocating for children and working women. She said she would push for more family leave and more rights for working mothers but, unsurprisingly, she hasn't done any of this. The U.S. is the wealthiest nation in the world, and yet, it ranks low globally in many metrics \-- especially in terms of childcare. We lack universal paid family leave, universal kindergarten, or affordable options for childcare. In fact, U.S. families use 23.1% of their income on childcare, compared to 11.8% for families in similar countries. Across the U.S., childcare options are dwindling, making affordable options for families few and far between \-- some childcare providers have 18-month waitlists and are no longer accepting vouchers and other financial assistance programs, further driving up the cost for American families. **[Early childcare is critical to a kid 's upbringing -- it allows them to jumpstart learning and sets them up to better succeed in school. Add your name to ours and tell Congress that it's time we supported American families; make childcare affordable for all Americans now!](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd06/6e9863e8/14ed0521/1db0dbc5/1691409228/VEsH/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/affordable-childcare/wdtop/")** Thank you for standing up for families across the country, The WDTO Team [SIGN THE PETITION](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd06/6e9863e8/14ed0521/1db0dbc5/1691409228/VEsE/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/affordable-childcare/wdtop/")

subject: I have a message for Susan Collins.
body:
Are you with me?
# [](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c3a/4bef7a87/68855866/287f37b5/969958558/VEsH/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_header")

Friends,
Every single day since my daughter Jaime was murdered in Parkland is another day I am fighting to end gun violence in America.
Unfortunately our legislators -- like Senator Susan Collins -- aren't doing the same.
I recently went to Capitol Hill to ask Senator Collins what she was doing to prevent any more Americans dying from senseless acts of gun violence. But instead of saying that she'd do everything in her power to stop this epidemic, she turned her back on me and walked away.
**So I have a message for Susan Collins: I 'm angry. I'm frustrated. And, like Americans across the country, I'm tired of waiting for you to do something. If you won't fight against gun violence, we'll elect someone who will.**
Her name is Sara Gideon, and she's going to defeat you in 2020. I'll make sure of it.
Are you with me?
**[Make a $3 contribution to Sara Gideon's campaign to defeat Susan Collins. Together, we can elect a Senate that won't turn its back on gun violence.](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c3a/4bef7a87/68855866/287f37b4/969958558/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTA5MjItZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-sept-deadline?refcode=em190922-full")**
Inaction is not an option. The longer we wait, the more lives like Jaime's that will be lost.
All my best,
Fred Guttenberg

* * *
Sara Gideon for Maine
PO Box 812
South Freeport, ME 04078

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: Susan Collins reaps what she sows
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Peter, Susan Collins was the deciding vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh to the Supreme Court, cutting short the already incomplete and limited investigation into his past \-- all this despite numerous allegations of sexual assault and misconduct against him. Senator Collins thought the anti-choice judge's "judicial temperament" and his potential to "lessen the divisions" in our government would be good for the Supreme Court, but instead, she voted for someone who lied to the Senate and then asked _New York Times_ reporters to lie for him. Now, all this is coming back to bite her \-- Maine voters are well aware that she betrayed her supposed pro-choice and pro-survivors values, and, in turn, betrayed Maine and America. Her net approval rating in Maine has dropped by 43 points in the past three years, showing how close she is to being voted out. Democrats have NO CHANCE of taking back the Senate unless we win in Maine. Speaker of the Maine House Sara Gideon is running to flip the seat blue, and if we're going to help her hold Susan Collins accountable for her callous vote to silence sexual assault survivors, we need to hear from 130 more engaged Democrats by MIDNIGHT: **Do you approve of Susan Collins?** [YES](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd08/6e9863e8/14eea033/1db0dbc9/3824343779/VEsH/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/q08kAHj1") [NO](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd08/6e9863e8/14eea033/1db0dbc9/3824343779/VEsE/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/q08kAHj1") Thanks for your support, WDTO Team [DONATE](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdd08/6e9863e8/14eea033/1db0dbc8/3824343779/VEsF/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sara-gideon-nd?refcode=WDTO20190922c.ND-b&refcode2=5Lvg3iTmNsMdPKi4p3y1")

name: Eric Hyers, Beshear/Coleman 2019 Campaign Manager
subject: Re: 44 Days Out: State of the Race Memo
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beshear/Coleman 2019
body:
With less than 45 days to go until Election Day, I wanted to share the state of the race and our plan to win.
|
---

| | | With less than 45 days to go until Election Day, I wanted to share the state of the race and our plan to win.

This is long but I hope you take a minute to read it. If you just have a second, the bottom line is: **_the strongest ground game in Kentucky 's history, huge endorsements, and a consistent and solid lead in the polls have Andy Beshear in the driver's seat with less than 45 days to go until Election Day._**

**[Click here to chip in $5 right now.](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557a65/ad6dc73/141f5c76/db79e17/83644784/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjI3NiJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-memo?refcode=20190922nd__276__&amount=5")**
---
The latest sign of energy behind the Beshear/Coleman campaign came last week when new fundraising numbers showed Andy outraised the sitting governor. This hardly ever happens. And just a day after fundraising reports were public, Beshear scored another big win with the endorsement of the Kentucky State Fraternal Order of Police.

**Growing Ground Game vs. Campaigning With Government Resources:**

Since the start of July, the Beshear-led coordinated campaign has **knocked on more than 400,000 doors** and opened up more than a dozen field offices throughout Kentucky.

Andy Beshear has made it a top priority to show up, listen, and meet Kentucky voters where they are. Since Fancy Farm, Andy has held **nearly 100 campaign events** across Kentucky, visiting counties like: Daviess, Jefferson, Hardin, Nelson, Meade, Laurel, Casey, Adair, Kenton, Boone, Boyd, Floyd, Jessamine, Clark, Bullitt, Fayette, Anderson, Pike, Perry, McCracken, Marshall, Hopkins, Franklin, Caldwell, Webster, Woodford, Mercer, Henderson, Warren, Carter, Powell, Trigg, Breathitt, Bourbon, and Madison.

In contrast, Matt Bevin has heavily relied on taxpayer-funded resources to carry his failing campaign. The governor holds partisan political events at the governor's mansion and has been under fire for refusing to fully disclose why he is taking a state plane across the country, even going so far as to say that "it's none of their business." when asked about coming clean to Kentuckians about where he is taking the taxpayer-funded plane.

**Race Boils Down to Kitchen Table Issues:**

While Andy Beshear and Jacqueline Coleman have been unveiling their "Kitchen Table Agenda" to create good-paying jobs for working families, the race has quickly boiled down to two major issues: **health care and public education**. **[Chip in $5 right now to make sure we have the resources to highlight these two issues as much as possible.](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557a65/ad6dc73/141f5c76/db79e17/83644784/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjI3NiJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-memo?refcode=20190922nd__276__&amount=5")**

On both major challenges, Andy Beshear has a clear record of standing with working families, while Matt Bevin supports harmful policies that would hurt Kentucky families.
* **Health Care** : Right now, health care is the only issue that is currently featured in paid television advertising from BOTH Democrats and Republicans. Matt Bevin still favors repealing the Affordable Care Act, which would devastate Medicaid expansion for hundreds of thousands of Kentuckians and remove vital protections for people with pre-existing conditions.
* **Public Education** : Andy Beshear and Jacqueline Coleman have Matt Bevin on the run when it comes to public education. They rolled out their "Stop the Bullying, Raise the Pay" plan to give every teacher a $2,000 raise. It's not surprising that the Associated Press recently said, "Public education grabbed the spotlight in Kentucky's race for governor."

Overall, Andy Beshear and Jacqueline Coleman are running on a positive vision to lift up Kentucky families, unveiling comprehensive policy priorities related to expanding access to affordable health care, jobs and health care for veterans, ethics, creating good-paying agritech and advanced manufacturing jobs, fixing our failing infrastructure, marketing Kentucky as a top-notch tourism destination, respecting workers, raising pay for teachers, equal pay, voting rights, and expanded gaming.

To date, Matt Bevin has not released a single policy plan this entire campaign.

**Democratic Unity and "Republicans For Beshear" vs. Bevin's GOP Civil War:**

Governor Bevin is locked in a nasty feud he started with his own lieutenant governor, who recently "vowed" to continue battling him in court. Bevin also insulted the roughly 40% of Republican primary voters who supported Robert Goforth in the May 21st primary when he said in August that his opponent's name is a "name so easily forgotten."

This GOP infighting driven by Matt Bevin is clearly a major problem for his campaign.

On the other hand, Beshear has picked up key endorsements, including from a sitting Republican state senator and the Kentucky State Fraternal Order of Police.

Thank you for taking the time to read this memo. Under Andy's leadership, we are running a substantive campaign based on voter engagement that lays out a real vision for Kentucky's future. We appreciate you being a part of this and we're going to need all of your help in the final stretch.

Campaigns aren't cheap and Matt Bevin can tap into his personal fortune at any time. **Pitch in today so we can continue to execute our plan.**

**[DONATE $5](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557a65/ad6dc73/141f5c76/db79e14/83644784/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjI3NiJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-memo?express_lane=true&refcode=20190922nd__276__&amount=5")**

Thanks,

Eric Hyers
Campaign Manager

Race Boils Down to Kitchen Table Issues: While Andy Beshear and Jacqueline Coleman have been unveiling their "Kitchen Table Agenda" to create good-paying jobs for working families, the race has quickly boiled down to two major issues: health care and public education. "" On both major challenges, Andy Beshear has a clear record of standing with working families, while Matt Bevin supports harmful policies that would hurt Kentucky families. Health Care : Right now, health care is the only issue that is currently featured in paid television advertising from BOTH Democrats and Republicans. Matt Bevin still favors repealing the Affordable Care Act, which would devastate Medicaid expansion for hundreds of thousands of Kentuckians and remove vital protections for people with pre-existing conditions. Public Education : Andy Beshear and Jacqueline Coleman have Matt Bevin on the run when it comes to public education. They rolled out their "Stop the Bullying, Raise the Pay" plan to give every teacher a $2,000 raise. It's not surprising that the Associated Press recently said, "Public education grabbed the spotlight in Kentucky's race for governor." Overall, Andy Beshear and Jacqueline Coleman are running on a positive vision to lift up Kentucky families, unveiling comprehensive policy priorities related to expanding access to affordable health care, jobs and health care for veterans, ethics, creating good-paying agritech and advanced manufacturing jobs, fixing our failing infrastructure, marketing Kentucky as a top-notch tourism destination, respecting workers, raising pay for teachers, equal pay, voting rights, and expanded gaming. To date, Matt Bevin has not released a single policy plan this entire campaign. Democratic Unity and "Republicans For Beshear" vs. Bevin's GOP Civil War: Governor Bevin is locked in a nasty feud he started with his own lieutenant governor, who recently "vowed" to continue battling him in court. Bevin also insulted the roughly 40% of Republican primary voters who supported Robert Goforth in the May 21st primary when he said in August that his opponent's name is a "name so easily forgotten." This GOP infighting driven by Matt Bevin is clearly a major problem for his campaign. On the other hand, Beshear has picked up key endorsements, including from a sitting Republican state senator and the Kentucky State Fraternal Order of Police. Thank you for taking the time to read this memo. Under Andy's leadership, we are running a substantive campaign based on voter engagement that lays out a real vision for Kentucky's future. We appreciate you being a part of this and we're going to need all of your help in the final stretch. Campaigns aren't cheap and Matt Bevin can tap into his personal fortune at any time. Pitch in today so we can continue to execute our plan. subject: BREAKING: debate thresholds to increase
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
The DNC is getting ready to introduce new qualifying thresholds for the upcoming debates in November. The problem? They won’t tell us what the new thresholds are.

| [](http://act.yang2020.com/go/68?t=1&akid=628%2E377263%2EUw7Xtb) **_"DNC set to squeeze Dem debate stage again”
\- POLITICO, 9/20_** [Chip in to help Andrew qualify for the next debate](https://act.yang2020.com/go/10841?t=5&akid=628%2E377263%2EUw7Xtb)
Friend --

The DNC is getting ready to introduce new qualifying thresholds for the upcoming debates in November. **The problem? They won’t tell us what the new thresholds are.**

We can assume that the requirements will include some combination of high polling and donation numbers. And we know the DNC is biased in favor of establishment candidates.

But if we raise enough money in the final days of September, we will be able to make a major statement that our campaign is for real. And if we do, we’re confident Andrew will be on that debate stage no matter what the qualifications are.

[Yang Gang, we need you to step up: Can you donate $10 or more right now to make sure Andrew qualifies for the DNC debate stage in November?](https://act.yang2020.com/go/10841?t=13&akid=628%2E377263%2EUw7Xtb) _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$5 »](https://act.yang2020.com/go/10841?t=12&amount=5&express_lane=true&akid=628%2E377263%2EUw7Xtb)

People started to notice our campaign after we qualified for the first debate -- and we’ve been rising in the polls ever since. We’re now polling in the top six and even the top four in some states.

But we know the establishment will do whatever they can to keep the focus on the reality TV show of our politics, and the media will keep covering the politicians they’ve grown accustomed to covering. That’s where you come in.

[We need to qualify for the November debate, no matter the threshold. Please chip in now to help Andrew get there.](https://act.yang2020.com/go/10841?t=6&akid=628%2E377263%2EUw7Xtb)
